summary: Problem 4.2 asks whether there exists a fixed $C>0$ such that $\log R_2(2,n) \leq (\log M_2(n))^C$, i.e., whether the two-dimensional Ramsey number $R_2(2,n)$ is bounded above by a fixed power of $\log M_2(n)$, the logarithm of the two-dimensional Erdős–Szekeres number. No follow-up paper addressing this specific question was found in the literature as of May 2026, some 16 months after the paper's publication in Discrete Analysis.
search_queries: Girão Kronenberg Scott "multidimensional Ramsey" R_2 M_2 two-dimensional Erdős–Szekeres
arXiv 2210.09227 Girão Kronenberg Scott two-dimensional Ramsey number Erdős–Szekeres follow-up
Girão Kronenberg Scott "multidimensional Ramsey" 2025 2026 two-dimensional problem open question
notes: No follow-up found in indexed literature. The conjecture concerns whether the singly-to-doubly exponential gap between $M_2(n)$ and $R_2(2,n)$ collapses in logarithmic terms; this remains open. The paper was published in Discrete Analysis (December 2024).
